PipelineRunStatusData.description:
`data` payload of GET /pipelines/{pipeline_id}/runs/{invocation_id}.

A small, stable projection of one `pipeline_run_logs` row. Optional fields
are dropped from the wire when absent (`exclude_none`) — a consumer treats a
missing field as "not recorded yet for this run" (an in-flight run has no
`finished_at` or counts), never as a defect. No internal infrastructure
detail is exposed.

error.description: Customer-safe failure category; present only for failed/partial runs.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the run reached a terminal state.
finished_at.title: Finished At

invocation_id.description: Unique identifier of this run (the id returned by the 202 accept).
invocation_id.title: Invocation Id

records_total.description: Total records read from the source.
records_total.title: Records Total

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the run began executing.
started_at.title: Started At

status.description: Coarse run status. Terminal: succeeded, partial, failed, cancelled.

submitted_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the run was accepted. Always present.
submitted_at.title: Submitted At

PipelineRunStatusData.title: PipelineRunStatusData

PublicErrorCode.description:
Customer-safe failure category. Mirrors the engine's `ErrorCode`
(analitiq-engine #258). `INTERNAL` is the catch-all for failures with no
engine-reported category (e.g. a failure before the engine started).
PublicErrorCode.title: PublicErrorCode

PublicRunError.description:
Customer-safe failure category for a run. Present only on `failed`
(always) and `partial` (when the engine reported a category).

code.description: Stable failure category. Mirrors the engine's error codes.

message.description: Fixed, customer-safe description of the failure category.
message.title: Message

PublicRunError.title: PublicRunError

PublicRunStatus.description:
Coarse, stable run status for external API-key consumers.

A deliberate reduction of the internal run lifecycle so the public contract
stays stable and infra-agnostic: the execution states we run on can change
without reshaping what an external consumer sees. The last four members are
terminal.
PublicRunStatus.title: PublicRunStatus

description: PUBLIC JSON Schema contract for the SUCCESS (200) response body of the Data Sync API GET /pipelines/{pipeline_id}/runs/{invocation_id} (rest.<domain>/v1, API-key auth) — the canonical `{success, message?, data}` envelope where `data` carries the run's public status: a coarse `status`, run timestamps, record counts, and — on failure — a customer-safe error category. A deliberately small, stable projection of the internal run-log that exposes no infrastructure detail (batch job ids, raw error strings, ...). A client that accepted a run (202) polls this by invocation_id for the outcome. Error responses use the canonical error envelope and are documented in the Data Sync OpenAPI rather than here. Absent optional fields are omitted from the wire, never null. Served at schemas.<domain> for external API-key consumers.

data.description: The run's public status — always present on a 200.

success.description: Always true on this 2xx body. Error responses use the canonical `{success: false, message, ...}` error envelope.
success.title: Success

title: Analitiq Data Sync API — Pipeline Run Status
